summary:
Claim: The correct order of epidermal layers from surface to deep is corneocytes > Merkel cells > melanocytes > Langerhans cells.  
Evidence: - Corneocytes are located in the stratum corneum, forming the outermost surface layer. - Merkel cells are situated in the stratum spinosum, just below the corneocytes; melanocytes and Langerhans cells are deeper, with melanocytes primarily in the stratum basale and Langerhans cells distributed throughout the stratum spinosum and more deeply.  
Answer: A
